channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: House Panel Supports BIR Compliance with Senate Subpoena in Duterte Impeachment Case
item.description: The House prosecution panel expressed that there is no valid justification for President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to block the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) from adhering to a Senate impeachment court ...

item.title: Suspect in Ann Widdecombe's murder charged with murder
item.description: Joshua Kerry, a 28-year-old former lift company worker, has been charged with the murder of Ann Widdecombe, a prominent Reform UK spokeswoman and former MP. The attack occurred on 8 July at her remote...

item.title: NPA Seizes R326m in Assets Linked to Tembisa Hospital Corruption Scandal
item.description: The National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) has been granted court approval to auction off R326m in assets linked to Hangwani Maumela, a tenderpreneur accused of orchestrating a R2bn looting scheme at Te...

item.title: Labour Party Successfully Uploads Candidates' Details to INEC Portal
item.description: The Labour Party (LP) has confirmed it received the access code needed to upload candidates' details for the 2027 governorship and State House of Assembly elections. National Publicity Secretary Ken A...

item.title: Nigerian Man Becomes Registered Nurse in UK at 64 After Career Change
item.description: John Adesoye, a 64-year-old Nigerian, has become a registered nurse in the UK after switching careers from electronics, inspiring others with his message that age is no barrier to learning. Before his...

item.title: JM and Fyang's Song Dedication List Revealed
item.description: The article highlights a curated list of songs that JM Ibarra and Fyang Smith, popular Filipino musicians known as JMFyang, would dedicate to each other. It emphasizes their collaborative work in the ...

item.title: Vancouver police manage record-breaking 2026 FIFA World Cup security operations
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Vancouver marked the city's largest-ever police deployment, with up to 1,300 officers stationed at BC Place Stadium across seven match days. Vancouver Police Department (VPD...

item.title: Suspect in 2023 Pickering Casino Homicide Arrested in Saskatoon
item.description: A 20-year-old man connected to a 2023 homicide at the Pickering Casino Resort in Ontario was arrested in Saskatoon, according to police. The suspect, who was wanted on a Canada-wide warrant, is accuse...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi criticises police action on NEET-UG protesters, says officials will be accountable under the Constitution
item.description: Congress MP and Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi criticised the Delhi Police after tear gas and lathi-charge were used against protesters marching towards Parliament over the alleged...

item.title: Tanzania's youth talents shine in sports development
item.description: The article highlights key sports developments in Tanzania, focusing on youth empowerment and innovative initiatives. CRDB Bank is commended for nurturing young talents through programs that provide t...

item.title: Kevin Keegan, English football legend, passes away at 75 after cancer battle
item.description: Kevin Keegan, a two-time Ballon d’Or winner and former England captain, has passed away at 75 after a courageous fight against stage four cancer. His death was announced by Newcastle United, his forme...

item.title: NPA Seizes R326m Assets Linked to Tembisa Hospital Corruption Scandal
item.description: The National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) has seized luxury properties and a high-value car collection belonging to Hangwani Maumela, a key figure implicated in the Tembisa Hospital looting scandal. Th...

item.title: Delhi Police Detain Protesters During CJP March, Claim They Are Not Students
item.description: Delhi Police have detained over 100 protesters during the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) march to Parliament, asserting that the detained individuals are not students but verified identities. A senior o...

item.title: Top 10 Funny Moments from World Cup 2026
item.description: The 2026 World Cup ended with Spain defeating Argentina to win their second title, but the tournament was filled with hilarious moments that became viral memes. From players' confused expressions afte...

item.title: Tanzania's climate project targets 30,000 residents in Kasulu
item.description: The article highlights a climate project in Tanzania targeting 30,000 residents in Kasulu, aiming to address environmental challenges. It also mentions the National Social Security Fund (NSSF) urging ...

item.title: Man convicted for assaulting child and grandmother in violent incident
item.description: A 45-year-old man from North West, South Africa, has been sentenced to 22 years in prison for attempting to kill his 10-month-old son and seriously injuring his 69-year-old grandmother. The incident o...

item.title: Philippines condemns China's aggression in West Philippine Sea incident
item.description: Akbayan Party-list Rep. Dadah Kiram Ismula condemned China's alleged violent actions against Philippine Navy personnel in the West Philippine Sea, comparing the Chinese Coast Guard to criminals who co...

item.title: Spotify introduces measures to combat AI-generated music on its platform
item.description: Spotify has introduced three key initiatives to address the growing issue of AI-generated music on its platform. The first is the 'Verified by Spotify' program, which displays a green checkmark on art...

item.title: Payroll Records Reveal Mary Habila and Anita Baaki Were University Staff Amid Ongoing Death Investigation
item.description: A fresh payroll document obtained by SaharaReporters confirms that Mary Habila and Anita Baaki were employed by David Umahi Federal University of Health Sciences (DUFUHS) and paid through the IPPIS pl...

item.title: Man arrested after incendiary device incident at ICE office in NYC
item.description: A man is in custody following an incident where he allegedly ignited an incendiary device outside a federal building housing the ICE field office in New York City. The event occurred around 8:30 a.m. ...
